evals.name: beach-puppy-playable
evals.prompt: make me a lingbot 2 prompt for a golden retriever puppy that you can drive around a sunny beach
evals.expected_output: A playable-character base prompt (camera behind/above, WASD clause, UE5-style anchor) plus actions.json with f, g, a few numbered event keys, and space, prompt_en = base + addendum
assertions: actions.json exists, is valid JSON, and its 'actions' array contains slots f, g, at least two numbered event keys (slots '1', '2', ...), and space, in that order
every action's prompt_en equals the base prompt + ' ' + addendum_en, joined verbatim (no paraphrase)
the space action is exactly 'The current controllable subject springs upward into the air.' with label 'Jump'
the base prompt contains an explicit motion contract tying all motion to key input (WASD / arrow-key clause with 'only while held' or 'in response to keyboard')

the f addendum is a contextual scene event opening with a subject-preservation clause when a new entity enters; the g addendum is a global environment/weather/lighting transformation naming specific scene surfaces
each numbered key is one discrete event; single-beat keys end on a settled note, and any transformation/teleport key ends in a new stable state rather than open-ended motion
no addendum restates the arrow-key camera/input contract (the f subject-preservation clause is the only allowed restatement)
the base prompt uses the playable-character regime: third-person gameplay video, camera follows from behind and slightly above, and a game-style anchor (e.g. Unreal Engine 5 style)

evals.name: lighthouse-keeper-orbit
evals.prompt: lingbot-world v2 session: an old lighthouse keeper standing at the edge of a cliff at dusk
evals.expected_output: An orbit-regime base prompt (subject remains at centre, arrow-key orbit around the stable subject only while held, 'With no event key pressed' idle clause) plus f, g, numbered keys, and space in the export JSON shape
assertions: actions.json exists, is valid JSON, and its 'actions' array contains slots f, g, at least two numbered event keys, and space, in that order
every action's prompt_en equals the base prompt + ' ' + addendum_en, joined verbatim (no paraphrase)
the space action is exactly 'The current controllable subject springs upward into the air.' with label 'Jump'

the base prompt uses the orbit regime with current phrasing: the subject 'remains at the exact centre of the frame at constant size and distance', arrow-key look-input orbiting around the stable subject 'only while held'
the base prompt ends with an idle clause ('With no event key pressed, ...') describing a specific interaction-ready rest pose and naming ambient details held still
the f addendum opens with the subject-preservation clause, and no addendum contradicts the base's idle clause except for the one thing it animates
no u or o slots are emitted (the user did not ask for the legacy combat layout)

evals.name: moon-astronaut-files
evals.prompt: need a lingbot 2 base prompt + actions.json for an astronaut on the moon, playable, low gravity. save the files to the output dir
evals.expected_output: prompt.txt and actions.json written as files; playable regime; f, g, numbered keys, space; valid JSON matching the export schema
assertions: actions.json exists, is valid JSON, and its 'actions' array contains slots f, g, at least two numbered event keys, and space, in that order
every action's prompt_en equals the base prompt + ' ' + addendum_en, joined verbatim (no paraphrase)
the space action is exactly 'The current controllable subject springs upward into the air.' with label 'Jump'
the base prompt contains an explicit motion contract tying all motion to key input (WASD / arrow-key clause with 'only while held' or 'in response to keyboard')
prompt.txt and actions.json are actually written as files in the outputs directory
the base prompt uses the playable-character regime and works low-gravity physics into the movement description

evals.name: ramen-shop-single-shot
evals.prompt: i just need a single lingbot 2 prompt, no action keys or events -- a cozy ramen shop interior at night
evals.expected_output: One standalone base prompt (first-person anchor regime for a place), with the motion contract anchored on a named foreground element; NO actions.json and no action keys
assertions: exactly one prompt is delivered and NO actions.json or action-key package is produced

the prompt uses the first-person anchor regime appropriate for a place: a named foreground anchor (e.g. the counter, a steaming bowl) that 'remains at the exact centre of the frame', with arrow-key look-input as the only source of camera motion 'only while held'
the prompt states what stays still with specifics: an idle/freeze clause naming two or three concrete scene elements held still (not a generic 'everything is static')
the prompt is one paragraph, present tense, roughly 60-110 words, and populates the scene with concrete secondary objects
the response does not pad the deliverable with unrequested extras (no action keys offered as files, no JSON)

evals.name: haunted-manor-chain
evals.prompt: lingbot 2 package: first-person with a lantern in a haunted manor hallway, and I want number keys that take you through an escalating sequence of ghost encounters
evals.expected_output: First-person anchor base prompt (lantern as anchor), f/g slots, and a chained sequence of English numbered keys where each key resolves the previous encounter before introducing the next
assertions: actions.json exists, is valid JSON, and contains f, g, at least three numbered event keys, and space, in that order
the base prompt anchors on the lantern (or an equivalent named foreground element) with the orbit-around-stable-anchor contract and a 'With no event key pressed' idle clause
all prompts and addenda are written in English (the user gave no language preference)
the numbered keys form a designed chain: every key after the first opens by resolving or dismissing the previous key's encounter before introducing the next
each chained key ends with the new encounter established in a stable state, not open-ended accelerating motion
every action's prompt_en equals the base prompt + ' ' + addendum_en joined verbatim
